title: Factory Task Contract v2
description: Canonical, signed cross-factory task contract (RFC-0002). Superset of AIFactory's implementation_plan.json adding execution + tfactory profiles. PFactory produces it; AIFactory executes it (skip planning); TFactory tests from it.

contract_version.description: Contract schema version. '2' enables the execution/tfactory blocks.

correlation_key.description: RFC-0001 correlation key: the GitHub issue number as a string, or a synthetic <prefix>-<id>.

approval.description: HMAC trust envelope (AIFactory trusted_plan). Required for the skip-planning fast-path.

signature.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex over the canonical contract + approval metadata.

required_commands.description: Global verification command NAMES granted into the security allowlist (uv/pytest/ruff/mypy/...).

depends_on.description: Phase numbers, or planner slugs (e.g. 'phase-1-scaffold'), this phase depends on.

execution.description: HOW to execute — computed by PFactory, consumed by AIFactory. Absent => AIFactory uses its own defaults/planning.

model.description: Default model (full id or shorthand opus/sonnet/haiku).

phase_models.description: Per-phase model overrides keyed by spec|planning|coding|qa|qa_fixer.

skip_planning.description: When true AND signature+completeness verify, AIFactory goes straight to the wave executor.

budget_usd.description: OPTIONAL soft cost budget in USD for the task. OBSERVE-ONLY: when AIFactory's rolled-up spend exceeds it, the terminal completion event carries a usage.budget warning block (limit_usd/spent_usd/exceeded) and an OTel budget.exceeded metric fires. NEVER aborts, pauses, or kills the build. Absent => no budget tracking (back-compat).

tfactory.description: VERIFY profile — computed by PFactory, carried by AIFactory, consumed by TFactory. Absent => TFactory infers.

frameworks.description: Lane -> framework, e.g. {unit: pytest, browser: playwright}.

docker_compose.description: Path to a compose file for integration lanes.

security_scope.description: Rule sets, e.g. owasp:* ; empty => out of scope.

valLevel.description: Verification Assurance Level (RFC-0006). VAL-0 static · VAL-1 unit · VAL-2 ephemeral integration · VAL-3 sandbox target · VAL-4 production (never autonomous).

verification.description: RFC-0006 honest-reporting block. A status is reported ONLY at achieved_level; every un-run/failed level carries a reason. A producer that omits this block is treated as VAL-0 (never 'tested'). The ordinal never-overclaim rule (no level marked 'passed' above achieved_level) is enforced by the verification gate (scripts/verification_gate.py), not expressible in JSON Schema alone.

reason.description: Why not passed — REQUIRED for failed/not_run/skipped.

risk.description: Plain-language consequence of this gap for the user.

claim.description: Mandatory honest one-liner stating what was and was NOT verified.

access.description: RFC-0007 access requirements discovered at planning time. Absent => the task needs no external/authenticated resource. The curation gate sets curated/human_approval; TFactory consumes credential_ref; the VAL gate (RFC-0006) treats an un-curated requirement as a VAL-3 not_run. Secrets are NEVER stored here — only broker refs.

requirements.description: One entry per external/authenticated resource the task's tests must reach.

auth_class.description: A: OIDC federation / service account / scoped token (no MFA in path). B: device-code / refresh token / TOTP-seed-as-secret / captured session (human clears MFA once). C: disposable target the pipeline owns (no prod cred). D: interactive MFA (push / hardware key / SMS) — refused or assisted, never faked.

credential_ref.description: Existing broker ref the test will use: env:NAME | vault:path#field | store:<id>. Never an inline secret.

env_required.description: Env var names the auth needs at runtime.

bootstrap.description: human => a one-time human-verified login/seed/approval is required before this is usable.

curated.description: Set true ONLY by the curation gate once the credential/target is provisioned and a liveness check passes. VAL-3 stays not_run until true.

human_approval.description: Audit trail of the human bootstrap (RFC-0001a chain).

scope.description: What was granted, e.g. 'sandbox account, read+deploy, auto-teardown'.

mvp_note.description: For auth_class D: the honest reason testing cannot pass this gate autonomously.

environment.description: RFC-0005 environment manifest — the single source of truth for the per-task toolchain, so the build env (AIFactory) and verify env (TFactory) cannot drift. The planner writes it; the provisioner materializes a throwaway sandbox from it (Tier A Nix preferred). Absent => the task runs in whatever the consumer's default runner provides.

toolchain.description: Pinned tool => version map, e.g. {"rust": "1.90", "cargo": "1.90"}. Versions feed the flake/devcontainer the provisioner builds.

system_packages.description: OS/Nix package names the tests need beyond the language toolchain, e.g. ['pkg-config', 'openssl-dev', 'chromium']. For a browser lane: the browser + its libs (e.g. via nixpkgs playwright-driver.browsers).

build_commands.description: Commands AIFactory runs to build the artifact in the materialized env.

verify_commands.description: Commands TFactory runs to verify in the SAME materialized env (no drift).

serve_command.description: How to start the app inside the materialized env for a lane that needs it running (browser/api against a live URL), e.g. 'python -m uvicorn app:app --port 8099'. Optional: when absent the consumer detects it from the checkout. Backgrounded by the runner, with a readiness wait, before the browser/api verify commands.

provisioning.description: How the consumer materializes this env. The provisioner resolves it tiered (A Nix, B image catalog, C on-demand build, D setup script).

method.description: nix => `nix develop -c` of `ref` (preferred, hermetic). devcontainer => devcontainer.json. image => a prebuilt registry image tag. setup_script => run `ref` inside the ephemeral container (last resort).

generated.description: true when the planner generated `ref` (and committed it as a deliverable) rather than the repo already carrying it.

network.description: Egress posture for the materialized sandbox. 'none' for hermetic build/unit; 'restricted' when the lane must reach a declared target (RFC-0007 access).

proof.description: Materialize-or-HALT evidence (RFC-0005 §3.4): commands that must succeed in the provisioned env before coding/verifying proceeds.

verify.description: Toolchain-presence checks, e.g. ['rustc --version', 'chromium --version']. A failure HALTs with a provisioning error, never a silent fallback.
